mechanism: Multi-group within-subjects behavioral experiment with factorial manipulation of stimulus diagnosticity and evaluation mode (online vs memory-based). Dependent variables measured on rating scales, analyzed via ANOVA and mediational path analysis. Key process: explicit causal inferences during judgment predict outcome differential.

Multi-sample correlational study with replication across independent assessment modalities. Three personality inventories administered to multiple participant cohorts; behavioral criteria measured via self-report and peer-rating convergent validity. Analysis: correlational and multiple regression predicting behavior from trait scores.

Multi-study measurement development and validation pipeline. Confirmatory factor analysis establishes latent structure; test-retest and means-and-covariance analyses confirm temporal stability; experience sampling methodology links trait to daily state; known-groups analysis discriminates patentees from alumni. Hierarchical integration of latent trait model across multiple timescales.

Three-arm experimental design with ego-depletion manipulation (attention control or emotion suppression) followed by cognitive performance tasks (GRE, CET, GMAT, reading comprehension, memory). Key process: prior self-regulation exhausts limited resource; resource sufficiency determines task success. Analysis: univariate and mixed-model ANOVA comparing performance across depletion conditions.

Between-subjects factorial experiments with joint vs separate evaluation modes. Participants predict happiness under one mode; control groups experience outcome under different mode. Core mechanism: utility function shape differs across evaluation contexts (steeper in JE, flatter in SE around reference point). Predictions vs experiences compared via t-tests and ANOVA.

Four-study design integrating actual exam analysis (eraser-mark coding) with experimental recall tasks and mediation modeling. Study 1: longitudinal archival data on answer changes. Studies 2-4: prospective measurement of affect (regret, frustration), memory reconstruction, and belief formation. Path analysis traces causal chain: action (switch) → affect → memory → belief.
